claim.summary: A live delegated queue drain was interrupted with SIGINT during a bounded external-manifest task, left the job resumable, then a fresh drain invocation resumed and completed it successfully.

focused_tests.result: passed: py_compile clean; 2 sandbox tests passed in 1.29s

code_changes: Added sleep to the sandbox host executable allowlist.
Added a regression proving sleep remains bounded by the sandbox timeout.

negative_probe.result: sleep was initially blocked by sandbox executable policy and produced a terminal safe_stop; this was treated as a real gap, not positive evidence.

open_limits: This is a single bounded real restart proof, not a long endurance run.
The workload is an external manifest task designed to be interruptible; broader non-fixture production workloads still need proof.
A stronger full A5 claim still needs repeated real restart/resume episodes over longer wall-clock operation.
